meta.title: Zone Mapping Rules — System Zoning Reference
meta.description: Rules for dividing fire alarm systems into zones for identification and response

purpose: Identify the general location of a fire for emergency responders
Enable phased evacuation in large buildings
Match annunciator display to building layout
zone_design_principles.fundamental_rule: Each zone should represent one identifiable fire compartment or area that can be quickly located by firefighters

bs5839_zoning.one_floor_per_zone: Ideally one zone should not cover more than one floor
bs5839_zoning.floor_area_exception: If a floor is less than 300m², it MAY share a zone with adjacent floor (with justification)
bs5839_zoning.max_search_distance: 60m — firefighter must not have to walk more than 60m within a zone to identify the fire location
bs5839_zoning.max_detectors_conventional: 20 detectors per zone (recommended limit for conventional systems)
bs5839_zoning.addressable_zones: Virtual zones in software — same area rules apply but devices can be grouped flexibly
bs5839_zoning.zone_identification: Each zone must have a unique number and clear label matching the floor plan

special_zoning.fire_doors: Fire doors should be in a separate zone from the area they protect
special_zoning.lift_shafts: Each lift shaft should be its own zone
special_zoning.stairwells: Enclosed stairwells should be separate zones from floor areas
special_zoning.plant_rooms: Boiler rooms, AHU rooms, generator rooms — separate zones
special_zoning.voids: Ceiling voids > 800mm should be zoned separately or included with the room below
special_zoning.toilets: Can be included with adjacent zone (if no detectors in toilet)
special_zoning.corridors: Should be in the same zone as the rooms they serve

zone_plan.requirement: Zone chart (floor plan) must be displayed adjacent to the control panel
zone_plan.format: Color-coded floor plan showing all zones with clear labels
zone_plan.scale: Must be large enough to read easily (minimum A3 size for typical buildings)

nfpa72_zoning.description: NFPA 72 does not prescribe specific zone limits like BS 5839, but provides guidance

conventional_zones.max_devices: Varies by manufacturer — typically 20-30 devices per zone
conventional_zones.guidance: Zone should represent a fire compartment or identifiable area
conventional_zones.floor_per_zone: Recommended practice — one zone per floor

addressable_zones.description: Zones are software-defined groups of addresses
addressable_zones.flexibility: Multiple zones per floor or single zone across multiple floors (if justified)
addressable_zones.mapping: Zone map must match floor plan at annunciator

annunciator.requirement: Remote annunciator at building entrance or fire command center
annunciator.display: LED or LCD display showing alarm/trouble/supervisory status per zone
annunciator.labels: Permanent labels matching building floor plan

zone_labeling_convention.description: Recommended zone naming for clear identification
zone_labeling_convention.notes: Always include floor number and area name — never just a number

placement_rules: Each device must belong to exactly one zone
Address must be unique within the system (addressable)
Location coordinates must satisfy spacing rules for device type
